title: lithos-loom plugin result.json
description: Atomic JSON file every plugin writes on exit. See docs/SPECIFICATION.md §5.

task_id.description: The Lithos task ID this run claimed.

exit_code.description: Process exit code. 0=succeeded, 1=generic failure, 20=bad input/config (do not retry), 30=interrupted by signal.

rounds.description: Optional. The implement/review round count the run reached (story-develop). The runner ignores it; develop attach reads it (offline / from the completion store) so a reaped or replayed run's terminal summary still names the round count.

worktree.description: Absolute path to the worktree the plugin used, or null if no worktree was created.

artifacts.description: Map of artifact key to path. Paths are relative to worktree when worktree is non-null, else absolute.

commits.description: Full SHAs of commits this run produced, in chronological order.

pr_url.description: URL of the pull request opened by post-approval delivery, when the run delivered one. Absent on runs that opened no PR. Lets an offline reader (develop attach) name the PR without querying GitHub/Lithos.

spawned_tasks.description: Lithos task IDs the plugin created (e.g. prd-decompose's child story tasks).

metadata_updates.description: Patch applied to task.metadata via lithos_task_update on success.

resume.description: Present only on status=interrupted runs that are worth re-dispatching (e.g. a provider usage limit). The runner schedules a re-claim + re-run at resume_after; session ids let the plugin resume its on-disk transcripts from the retained work dir.

resume_after.description: Earliest instant a re-dispatch is expected to succeed (provider reset time, or a fallback delay when unknown).

reviewer_sessions.description: Map of reviewer name to its session id.
